# crowci_repository_cron_job (Resource)

Manages a cron job for a repository on the Crow CI server.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "crowci_repository_cron_job" "example" {
  repo_id  = 42
  name     = "nightly-build"
  schedule = "0 2 * * *"
  branch   = "main"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String) Cron job name.
- `repo_id` (Number) ID of the repository this cron job belongs to.
- `schedule` (String) Cron schedule expression (e.g. '@daily', '0 0 * * *').

### Optional

- `branch` (String) Branch to run the cron job on. Defaults to the repository's default branch.

### Read-Only

- `created` (Number) Creation time as a Unix timestamp.
- `creator_id` (Number) ID of the user who created this cron job.
- `disabled` (Boolean) Whether the cron job is disabled.
- `fail_count` (Number) Number of consecutive failures.
- `fail_msg` (String) Last failure message.
- `id` (Number) Cron job ID assigned by Crow CI.
- `next_exec` (Number) Next scheduled execution time as a Unix timestamp.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

In Terraform v1.12.0 and later, the [`import` block](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/import) can be used with the `identity` attribute, for example:

```terraform
import {
  to = crowci_repository_cron_job.example
  identity = {
    repo_id = 42
    id      = 7
  }
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
### Identity Schema

#### Required

- `id` (Number) Cron job ID.
- `repo_id` (Number) ID of the repository this cron job belongs to.

In Terraform v1.5.0 and later, the [`import` block](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/import) can be used with the `id` attribute, for example:

```terraform
import {
  to = crowci_repository_cron_job.example
  id = "42/7"
}
```

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
terraform import crowci_repository_cron_job.example 42/7
```
